cast\\u003cScalableVectorType\\u003e(\\n674:         VectorType::getSubdividedVectorType(VTy, NumSubdivs));\\n675:   }\\n676: \\n677:   static ScalableVectorType *\\n678:   getHalfElementsVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\\n679:     return cast\\u003cScalableVectorType\\u003e(VectorType::getHalfElementsVectorType(VTy));\\n680:   }\\n681: \\n682:   static ScalableVectorType *\\n683:   getDoubleElementsVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\\n684:     return cast\\u003cScalableVectorType\\u003e(\\n685:         VectorType::getDoubleElementsVectorType(VTy));\\n686:   }\\n687: \\n688:   /// Get the minimum number of elements in this vector. The actual number of\\n689:   /// elements in the vector is an integer multiple of this value.\\n690:   unsigned getMinNumElements() const { return ElementQuantity; }\\n691: \\n692:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\\n693:     return T-\\u003egetTypeID() == ScalableVectorTyID;\\n694:   }\\n695: };\\n696: \\n697: inline ElementCount VectorType::getElementCount() const {\\n698:   return ElementCount::get(ElementQuantity, isa\\u003cScalableVectorType\\u003e(this));\\n699: }\\n700: \\n701: /// Class to represent pointers.\\n702: class PointerType : public Type {\\n703:   explicit PointerType(LLVMContext \\u0026C, unsigned AddrSpace);\\n704: \\n705: public:\\n706:   PointerType(const PointerType \\u0026) = delete;\\n707:   PointerType \\u0026operator=(const PointerType \\u0026) = delete;\\n708: \\n709:   /// This constructs a pointer to an object of the specified type in a numbered\\n710:   /// address space.\\n711:   [[deprecated(\\\"PointerType::get with pointee type is pending removal. Use \\\"\\n712:                \\\"Context overload.\\\")]]\\n713:   LLVM_ABI static PointerType *get(Type *ElementType, unsigned AddressSpace);\\n714:   /// This constructs an opaque pointer to an object in a numbered address\\n715:   /// space.\\n716:   LLVM_ABI static PointerType *get(LLVMContext \\u0026C, unsigned AddressSpace);\\n717: \\n718:   /// This constructs a pointer to an object of the specified type in the\\n719:   /// default address space (address space zero).\\n720:   [[deprecated(\\\"PointerType::getUnqual with pointee type is pending removal. \\\"\\n721:                \\\"Use Context overload.\\\")]]\\n722:   static PointerType *getUnqual(Type *ElementType) {\\n723:     assert(ElementType \\u0026\\u0026 \\\"Can't get a pointer to \\u003cnull\\u003e type!\\\");\\n724:     assert(isValidElementType(ElementType) \\u0026\\u0026\\n725:            \\\"Invalid type for pointer element!\\\");\\n726:     return PointerType::getUnqual(ElementType-\\u003egetContext());\\n727:   }\\n728: \\n729:   /// This constructs an opaque pointer to an object in the\\n730:   /// default address space (address space zero).\\n731:   static PointerType *getUnqual(LLVMContext \\u0026C) {\\n732:     return PointerType::get(C, 0);\\n733:   }\\n734: \\n735:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a element type.\\n736:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidElementType(Type *ElemTy);\\n737: \\n738:   /// Return true if we can load or store from a pointer to this type.\\n739:   LLVM_ABI static bool isLoadableOrStorableType(Type *ElemTy);\\n740: \\n741:   /// Return the address space of the Pointer type.\\n742:   inline unsigned getAddressSpace() const { return getSubclassData(); }\\n743: \\n744:   /// Implement support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\\n745:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\\n746:     return T-\\u003egetTypeID() == PointerTyID;\\n747:   }\\n748: };\\n749: \\n750: Type *Type::getExtendedType() const {\\n751:   assert(\\n752:       isIntOrIntVectorTy() \\u0026\\u0026\\n753:       \\\"Original type expected to be a vector of integers or a scalar integer.\\\");\\n754:   if (auto *VTy = dyn_cast\\u003cVectorType\\u003e(this))\\n755:     return VectorType::getExtendedElementVectorType(\\n756:         const_cast\\u003cVectorType *\\u003e(VTy));\\n757:   return cast\\u003cIntegerType\\u003e(this)-\\u003egetExtendedType();\\n758: }\\n759: \\n760: Type *Type::getWithNewType(Type *EltTy) const {\\n761:   if (auto *VTy = dyn_cast\\u003cVectorType\\u003e(this))\\n762:     return VectorType::get(EltTy, VTy-\\u003egetElementCount());\\n763:   return EltTy;\\n764: }\\n765: \\n766: Type *Type::getWithNewBitWidth(unsigned NewBitWidth) const {\\n767:   assert(\\n768:       isIntOrIntVectorTy() \\u0026\\u0026\\n769:       \\\"Original type expected to be a vector of integers or a scalar integer.\\\");\\n770:   return getWithNewType(getIntNTy(getContext(), NewBitWidth));\\n771: }\\n772: \\n773: unsigned Type::getPointerAddressSpace() const {\\n774:   return cast\\u003cPointerType\\u003e(getScalarType())-\\u003egetAddressSpace();\\n775: }\\n776: \\n777: /// Class to represent target extensions types, which are generally\\n778: /// unintrospectable from target-independent optimizations.\\n779: ///\\n780: /// Target extension types have a string name, and optionally have type and/or\\n781: /// integer parameters. The exact meaning of any parameters is dependent on the\\n782: /// target.\\n783: class TargetExtType : public Type {\\n784:   TargetExtType(LLVMContext \\u0026C, StringRef Name, ArrayRef\\u003cType *\\u003e Types,\\n785:                 ArrayRef\\u003cunsigned\\u003e Ints);\\n786: \\n787:   // These strings are ultimately owned by the context.\\n788:   StringRef Name;\\n789:   unsigned *IntParams;\\n790: \\n791: public:\\n792:   TargetExtType(const TargetExtType \\u0026) = delete;\\n793:   TargetExtType \\u0026operator=(const TargetExtType \\u0026) = delete;\\n794: \\n795:   /// Return a target extension type having the specified name and optional\\n796:   /// type and integer parameters.\\n797:   LLVM_ABI static TargetExtType *get(LLVMContext \\u0026Context, StringRef Name,\\n798:                                      ArrayRef\\u003cType *\\u003e Types = {},\\n799:                                      ArrayRef\\u003cunsigned\\u003e Ints = {});\\n800: \\n801:   /// Return a target extension type having the specified name and optional\\n802:   /// type and integer parameters, or an appropriate Error if it fails the\\n803:   /// parameters check.\\n804:   LLVM_ABI static Expected\\u003cTargetExtType *\\u003e\\n805:   getOrError(LLVMContext \\u0026Context, StringRef Name, ArrayRef\\u003cType *\\u003e Types = {},\\n806:              ArrayRef\\u003cunsigned\\u003e Ints = {});\\n807: \\n808:   /// Check that a newly created target extension type has the expected number\\n809:   /// of type parameters and integer parameters, returning the type itself if OK\\n810:   /// or an appropriate Error if not.\\n811:   LLVM_ABI static Expected\\u003cTargetExtType *\\u003e checkParams(TargetExtType *TTy);\\n812: \\n813:   /// Return the name for this target extension type. Two distinct target\\n814:   /// extension types may have the same name if their type or integer parameters\\n815:   /// differ.\\n816:   StringRef getName() const { return Name; }\\n817: \\n818:   /// Return the type parameters for this particular target extension type. If\\n819:   /// there are no parameters, an empty array is returned.\\n820:   ArrayRef\\u003cType *\\u003e type_params() const {\\n821:     return ArrayRef(type_param_begin(), type_param_end());\\n822:   }\\n823: \\n824:   using type_param_iterator = Type::subtype_iterator;\\n825:   type_param_iterator type_param_begin() const { return ContainedTys; }\\n826:   type_param_iterator type_param_end() const {\\n827:     return \\u0026ContainedTys[NumContainedTys];\\n828:   }\\n829: \\n830:   Type *getTypeParameter(unsigned i) const { return getContainedType(i); }\\n831:   unsigned getNumTypeParameters() const { return getNumContainedTypes(); }\\n832: \\n833:   /// Return the integer parameters for this particular target extension type.\\n834:   /// If there are no parameters, an empty array is returned.\\n835:   ArrayRef\\u003cunsigned\\u003e int_params() const {\\n836:     return ArrayRef(IntParams, getNumIntParameters());\\n837:   }\\n838: \\n839:   unsigned getIntParameter(unsigned i) const { return IntParams[i]; }\\n840:   unsigned getNumIntParameters() const { return getSubclassData(); }\\n841: \\n842:   enum Property {\\n843:     /// zeroinitializer is valid for this target extension type.\\n844:     HasZeroInit = 1U \\u003c\\u003c 0,\\n845:     /// This type may be used as the value type of a global variable.\\n846:     CanBeGlobal = 1U \\u003c\\u003c 1,\\n847:     /// This type may be allocated on the stack, either as the allocated type\\n848:     /// of an alloca instruction or as a byval function parameter.\\n849:     CanBeLocal = 1U \\u003c\\u003c 2,\\n850:     /// This type may be used as an element in a vector.\\n851:     CanBeVectorElement = 1U \\u003c\\u003c 3,\\n852:     // This type can only be used in intrinsic arguments and return values.\\n853:     /// In particular, it cannot be used in select and phi instructions.\\n854:     IsTokenLike = 1U \\u003c\\u003c 4,\\n855:   };\\n856: \\n857:   /// Returns true if the target extension type contains the given property.\\n858:   LLVM_ABI bool hasProperty(Property Prop) const;\\n859: \\n860:   /// Returns an underlying layout type for the target extension type. This\\n861:   /// type can be used to query size and alignment information, if it is\\n862:   /// appropriate (although note that the layout type may also be void). It is\\n863:   /// not legal to bitcast between this type and the layout type, however.\\n864:   LLVM_ABI Type *getLayoutType() const;\\n865: \\n866:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\\n867:   static bool classof(const Type *T) { return T-\\u003egetTypeID() == TargetExtTyID; }\\n868: };\\n869: \\n870: StringRef Type::getTargetExtName() const {\\n871:   return cast\\u003cTargetExtType\\u003e(this)-\\u003egetName();\\n872: }\\n873: \\n874: } // end namespace llvm\\n875: \\n876: #endif // LLVM_IR_DERIVEDTYPES_H\\n877: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h\"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===- llvm/DerivedTypes.h - Classes for handling data types ----*- C++ -*-===//\n2: //\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\n6: //\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n8: //\n9: // This file contains the declarations of classes that represent \"derived\n10: // types\".  These are things like \"arrays of x\" or \"structure of x, y, z\" or\n11: // \"function returning x taking (y,z) as parameters\", etc...\n12: //\n13: // The implementations of these classes live in the Type.cpp file.\n14: //\n15: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n16: \n17: #ifndef LLVM_IR_DERIVEDTYPES_H\n18: #define LLVM_IR_DERIVEDTYPES_H\n19: \n20: #include \"llvm/ADT/ArrayRef.h\"\n21: #include \"llvm/ADT/STLExtras.h\"\n22: #include \"llvm/ADT/StringRef.h\"\n23: #include \"llvm/IR/Type.h\"\n24: #include \"llvm/Support/Casting.h\"\n25: #include \"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\"\n26: #include \"llvm/Support/TypeSize.h\"\n27: #include <cassert>\n28: #include <cstdint>\n29: \n30: namespace llvm {\n31: \n32: class Value;\n33: class APInt;\n34: class LLVMContext;\n35: template <typename T> class Expected;\n36: class Error;\n37: \n38: /// Class to represent integer types. Note that this class is also used to\n39: /// represent the built-in integer types: Int1Ty, Int8Ty, Int16Ty, Int32Ty and\n40: /// Int64Ty.\n41: /// Integer representation type\n42: class IntegerType : public Type {\n43:   friend class LLVMContextImpl;\n44: \n45: protected:\n46:   explicit IntegerType(LLVMContext &C, unsigned NumBits) : Type(C, IntegerTyID){\n47:     setSubclassData(NumBits);\n48:   }\n49: \n50: public:\n51:   /// This enum is just used to hold constants we need for IntegerType.\n52:   enum {\n53:     MIN_INT_BITS = 1,        ///< Minimum number of bits that can be specified\n54:     MAX_INT_BITS = (1<<23)   ///< Maximum number of bits that can be specified\n55:       ///< Note that bit width is stored in the Type classes SubclassData field\n56:       ///< which has 24 bits. SelectionDAG type legalization can require a\n57:       ///< power of 2 IntegerType, so limit to the largest representable power\n58:       ///< of 2, 8388608.\n59:   };\n60: \n61:   /// This static method is the primary way of constructing an IntegerType.\n62:   /// If an IntegerType with the same NumBits value was previously instantiated,\n63:   /// that instance will be returned. Otherwise a new one will be created. Only\n64:   /// one instance with a given NumBits value is ever created.\n65:   /// Get or create an IntegerType instance.\n66:   LLVM_ABI static IntegerType *get(LLVMContext &C, unsigned NumBits);\n67: \n68:   /// Returns type twice as wide the input type.\n69:   IntegerType *getExtendedType() const {\n70:     return Type::getIntNTy(getContext(), 2 * getScalarSizeInBits());\n71:   }\n72: \n73:   /// Get the number of bits in this IntegerType\n74:   unsigned getBitWidth() const { return getSubclassData(); }\n75: \n76:   /// Return a bitmask with ones set for all of the bits that can be set by an\n77:   /// unsigned version of this type. This is 0xFF for i8, 0xFFFF for i16, etc.\n78:   uint64_t getBitMask() const {\n79:     return ~uint64_t(0UL) >> (64-getBitWidth());\n80:   }\n81: \n82:   /// Return a uint64_t with just the most significant bit set (the sign bit, if\n83:   /// the value is treated as a signed number).\n84:   uint64_t getSignBit() const {\n85:     return 1ULL << (getBitWidth()-1);\n86:   }\n87: \n88:   /// For example, this is 0xFF for an 8 bit integer, 0xFFFF for i16, etc.\n89:   /// @returns a bit mask with ones set for all the bits of this type.\n90:   /// Get a bit mask for this type.\n91:   LLVM_ABI APInt getMask() const;\n92: \n93:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n94:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n95:     return T->getTypeID() == IntegerTyID;\n96:   }\n97: };\n98: \n99: unsigned Type::getIntegerBitWidth() const {\n100:   return cast<IntegerType>(this)->getBitWidth();\n101: }\n102: \n103: /// Class to represent function types\n104: ///\n105: class FunctionType : public Type {\n106:   FunctionType(Type *Result, ArrayRef<Type*> Params, bool IsVarArgs);\n107: \n108: public:\n109:   FunctionType(const FunctionType &) = delete;\n110:   FunctionType &operator=(const FunctionType &) = delete;\n111: \n112:   /// This static method is the primary way of constructing a FunctionType.\n113:   LLVM_ABI static FunctionType *get(Type *Result, ArrayRef<Type *> Params,\n114:                                     bool isVarArg);\n115: \n116:   /// Create a FunctionType taking no parameters.\n117:   LLVM_ABI static FunctionType *get(Type *Result, bool isVarArg);\n118: \n119:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a return type.\n120:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidReturnType(Type *RetTy);\n121: \n122:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as an argument type.\n123:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidArgumentType(Type *ArgTy);\n124: \n125:   bool isVarArg() const { return getSubclassData()!=0; }\n126:   Type *getReturnType() const { return ContainedTys[0]; }\n127: \n128:   using param_iterator = Type::subtype_iterator;\n129: \n130:   param_iterator param_begin() const { return ContainedTys + 1; }\n131:   param_iterator param_end() const { return &ContainedTys[NumContainedTys]; }\n132:   ArrayRef<Type *> params() const {\n133:     return ArrayRef(param_begin(), param_end());\n134:   }\n135: \n136:   /// Parameter type accessors.\n137:   Type *getParamType(unsigned i) const {\n138:     assert(i < getNumParams() && \"getParamType() out of range!\");\n139:     return ContainedTys[i + 1];\n140:   }\n141: \n142:   /// Return the number of fixed parameters this function type requires.\n143:   /// This does not consider varargs.\n144:   unsigned getNumParams() const { return NumContainedTys - 1; }\n145: \n146:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n147:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n148:     return T->getTypeID() == FunctionTyID;\n149:   }\n150: };\n151: static_assert(alignof(FunctionType) >= alignof(Type *),\n152:               \"Alignment sufficient for objects appended to FunctionType\");\n153: \n154: bool Type::isFunctionVarArg() const {\n155:   return cast<FunctionType>(this)->isVarArg();\n156: }\n157: \n158: Type *Type::getFunctionParamType(unsigned i) const {\n159:   return cast<FunctionType>(this)->getParamType(i);\n160: }\n161: \n162: unsigned Type::getFunctionNumParams() const {\n163:   return cast<FunctionType>(this)->getNumParams();\n164: }\n165: \n166: /// A handy container for a FunctionType+Callee-pointer pair, which can be\n167: /// passed around as a single entity. This assists in replacing the use of\n168: /// PointerType::getElementType() to access the function's type, since that's\n169: /// slated for removal as part of the [opaque pointer types] project.\n170: class FunctionCallee {\n171: public:\n172:   // Allow implicit conversion from types which have a getFunctionType member\n173:   // (e.g. Function and InlineAsm).\n174:   template <typename T, typename U = decltype(&T::getFunctionType)>\n175:   FunctionCallee(T *Fn)\n176:       : FnTy(Fn ? Fn->getFunctionType() : nullptr), Callee(Fn) {}\n177: \n178:   FunctionCallee(FunctionType *FnTy, Value *Callee)\n179:       : FnTy(FnTy), Callee(Callee) {\n180:     assert((FnTy == nullptr) == (Callee == nullptr));\n181:   }\n182: \n183:   FunctionCallee(std::nullptr_t) {}\n184: \n185:   FunctionCallee() = default;\n186: \n187:   FunctionType *getFunctionType() { return FnTy; }\n188: \n189:   Value *getCallee() { return Callee; }\n190: \n191:   explicit operator bool() { return Callee; }\n192: \n193: private:\n194:   FunctionType *FnTy = nullptr;\n195:   Value *Callee = nullptr;\n196: };\n197: \n198: /// Class to represent struct types. There are two different kinds of struct\n199: /// types: Literal structs and Identified structs.\n200: ///\n201: /// Literal struct types (e.g. { i32, i32 }) are uniqued structurally, and must\n202: /// always have a body when created.  You can get one of these by using one of\n203: /// the StructType::get() forms.\n204: ///\n205: /// Identified structs (e.g. %foo or %42) may optionally have a name and are not\n206: /// uniqued.  The names for identified structs are managed at the LLVMContext\n207: /// level, so there can only be a single identified struct with a given name in\n208: /// a particular LLVMContext.  Identified structs may also optionally be opaque\n209: /// (have no body specified).  You get one of these by using one of the\n210: /// StructType::create() forms.\n211: ///\n212: /// Independent of what kind of struct you have, the body of a struct type are\n213: /// laid out in memory consecutively with the elements directly one after the\n214: /// other (if the struct is packed) or (if not packed) with padding between the\n215: /// elements as defined by DataLayout (which is required to match what the code\n216: /// generator for a target expects).\n217: ///\n218: class StructType : public Type {\n219:   StructType(LLVMContext &C) : Type(C, StructTyID) {}\n220: \n221:   enum {\n222:     /// This is the contents of the SubClassData field.\n223:     SCDB_HasBody = 1,\n224:     SCDB_Packed = 2,\n225:     SCDB_IsLiteral = 4,\n226:     SCDB_IsSized = 8,\n227:     SCDB_ContainsScalableVector = 16,\n228:     SCDB_NotContainsScalableVector = 32,\n229:     SCDB_ContainsNonGlobalTargetExtType = 64,\n230:     SCDB_NotContainsNonGlobalTargetExtType = 128,\n231:     SCDB_ContainsNonLocalTargetExtType = 64,\n232:     SCDB_NotContainsNonLocalTargetExtType = 128,\n233:   };\n234: \n235:   /// For a named struct that actually has a name, this is a pointer to the\n236:   /// symbol table entry (maintained by LLVMContext) for the struct.\n237:   /// This is null if the type is an literal struct or if it is a identified\n238:   /// type that has an empty name.\n239:   void *SymbolTableEntry = nullptr;\n240: \n241: public:\n242:   StructType(const StructType &) = delete;\n243:   StructType &operator=(const StructType &) = delete;\n244: \n245:   /// This creates an identified struct.\n246:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(LLVMContext &Context, StringRef Name);\n247:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(LLVMContext &Context);\n248: \n249:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(ArrayRef<Type *> Elements, StringRef Name,\n250:                                      bool isPacked = false);\n251:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(ArrayRef<Type *> Elements);\n252:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(LLVMContext &Context,\n253:                                      ArrayRef<Type *> Elements, StringRef Name,\n254:                                      bool isPacked = false);\n255:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *create(LLVMContext &Context,\n256:                                      ArrayRef<Type *> Elements);\n257:   template <class... Tys>\n258:   static std::enable_if_t<are_base_of<Type, Tys...>::value, StructType *>\n259:   create(StringRef Name, Type *elt1, Tys *... elts) {\n260:     assert(elt1 && \"Cannot create a struct type with no elements with this\");\n261:     return create(ArrayRef<Type *>({elt1, elts...}), Name);\n262:   }\n263: \n264:   /// This static method is the primary way to create a literal StructType.\n265:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *\n266:   get(LLVMContext &Context, ArrayRef<Type *> Elements, bool isPacked = false);\n267: \n268:   /// Create an empty structure type.\n269:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *get(LLVMContext &Context, bool isPacked = false);\n270: \n271:   /// This static method is a convenience method for creating structure types by\n272:   /// specifying the elements as arguments. Note that this method always returns\n273:   /// a non-packed struct, and requires at least one element type.\n274:   template <class... Tys>\n275:   static std::enable_if_t<are_base_of<Type, Tys...>::value, StructType *>\n276:   get(Type *elt1, Tys *... elts) {\n277:     assert(elt1 && \"Cannot create a struct type with no elements with this\");\n278:     LLVMContext &Ctx = elt1->getContext();\n279:     return StructType::get(Ctx, ArrayRef<Type *>({elt1, elts...}));\n280:   }\n281: \n282:   /// Return the type with the specified name, or null if there is none by that\n283:   /// name.\n284:   LLVM_ABI static StructType *getTypeByName(LLVMContext &C, StringRef Name);\n285: \n286:   bool isPacked() const { return (getSubclassData() & SCDB_Packed) != 0; }\n287: \n288:   /// Return true if this type is uniqued by structural equivalence, false if it\n289:   /// is a struct definition.\n290:   bool isLiteral() const { return (getSubclassData() & SCDB_IsLiteral) != 0; }\n291: \n292:   /// Return true if this is a type with an identity that has no body specified\n293:   /// yet. These prints as 'opaque' in .ll files.\n294:   bool isOpaque() const { return (getSubclassData() & SCDB_HasBody) == 0; }\n295: \n296:   /// isSized - Return true if this is a sized type.\n297:   LLVM_ABI bool isSized(SmallPtrSetImpl<Type *> *Visited = nullptr) const;\n298: \n299:   /// Returns true if this struct contains a scalable vector.\n300:   LLVM_ABI bool isScalableTy(SmallPtrSetImpl<const Type *> &Visited) const;\n301:   using Type::isScalableTy;\n302: \n303:   /// Return true if this type is or contains a target extension type that\n304:   /// disallows being used as a global.\n305:   LLVM_ABI bool\n306:   containsNonGlobalTargetExtType(SmallPtrSetImpl<const Type *> &Visited) const;\n307:   using Type::containsNonGlobalTargetExtType;\n308: \n309:   /// Return true if this type is or contains a target extension type that\n310:   /// disallows being used as a local.\n311:   LLVM_ABI bool\n312:   containsNonLocalTargetExtType(SmallPtrSetImpl<const Type *> &Visited) const;\n313:   using Type::containsNonLocalTargetExtType;\n314: \n315:   /// Returns true if this struct contains homogeneous scalable vector types.\n316:   /// Note that the definition of homogeneous scalable vector type is not\n317:   /// recursive here. That means the following structure will return false\n318:   /// when calling this function.\n319:   /// {{<vscale x 2 x i32>, <vscale x 4 x i64>},\n320:   ///  {<vscale x 2 x i32>, <vscale x 4 x i64>}}\n321:   LLVM_ABI bool containsHomogeneousScalableVectorTypes() const;\n322: \n323:   /// Return true if this struct is non-empty and all element types are the\n324:   /// same.\n325:   LLVM_ABI bool containsHomogeneousTypes() const;\n326: \n327:   /// Return true if this is a named struct that has a non-empty name.\n328:   bool hasName() const { return SymbolTableEntry != nullptr; }\n329: \n330:   /// Return the name for this struct type if it has an identity.\n331:   /// This may return an empty string for an unnamed struct type.  Do not call\n332:   /// this on an literal type.\n333:   LLVM_ABI StringRef getName() const;\n334: \n335:   /// Change the name of this type to the specified name, or to a name with a\n336:   /// suffix if there is a collision. Do not call this on an literal type.\n337:   LLVM_ABI void setName(StringRef Name);\n338: \n339:   /// Specify a body for an opaque identified type, which must not make the type\n340:   /// recursive.\n341:   LLVM_ABI void setBody(ArrayRef<Type *> Elements, bool isPacked = false);\n342: \n343:   /// Specify a body for an opaque identified type or return an error if it\n344:   /// would make the type recursive.\n345:   LLVM_ABI Error setBodyOrError(ArrayRef<Type *> Elements,\n346:                                 bool isPacked = false);\n347: \n348:   /// Return an error if the body for an opaque identified type would make it\n349:   /// recursive.\n350:   LLVM_ABI Error checkBody(ArrayRef<Type *> Elements);\n351: \n352:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a element type.\n353:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidElementType(Type *ElemTy);\n354: \n355:   // Iterator access to the elements.\n356:   using element_iterator = Type::subtype_iterator;\n357: \n358:   element_iterator element_begin() const { return ContainedTys; }\n359:   element_iterator element_end() const { return &ContainedTys[NumContainedTys];}\n360:   ArrayRef<Type *> elements() const {\n361:     return ArrayRef(element_begin(), element_end());\n362:   }\n363: \n364:   /// Return true if this is layout identical to the specified struct.\n365:   LLVM_ABI bool isLayoutIdentical(StructType *Other) const;\n366: \n367:   /// Random access to the elements\n368:   unsigned getNumElements() const { return NumContainedTys; }\n369:   Type *getElementType(unsigned N) const {\n370:     assert(N < NumContainedTys && \"Element number out of range!\");\n371:     return ContainedTys[N];\n372:   }\n373:   /// Given an index value into the type, return the type of the element.\n374:   LLVM_ABI Type *getTypeAtIndex(const Value *V) const;\n375:   Type *getTypeAtIndex(unsigned N) const { return getElementType(N); }\n376:   LLVM_ABI bool indexValid(const Value *V) const;\n377:   bool indexValid(unsigned Idx) const { return Idx < getNumElements(); }\n378: \n379:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n380:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n381:     return T->getTypeID() == StructTyID;\n382:   }\n383: };\n384: \n385: StringRef Type::getStructName() const {\n386:   return cast<StructType>(this)->getName();\n387: }\n388: \n389: unsigned Type::getStructNumElements() const {\n390:   return cast<StructType>(this)->getNumElements();\n391: }\n392: \n393: Type *Type::getStructElementType(unsigned N) const {\n394:   return cast<StructType>(this)->getElementType(N);\n395: }\n396: \n397: /// Class to represent array types.\n398: class ArrayType : public Type {\n399:   /// The element type of the array.\n400:   Type *ContainedType;\n401:   /// Number of elements in the array.\n402:   uint64_t NumElements;\n403: \n404:   ArrayType(Type *ElType, uint64_t NumEl);\n405: \n406: public:\n407:   ArrayType(const ArrayType &) = delete;\n408:   ArrayType &operator=(const ArrayType &) = delete;\n409: \n410:   uint64_t getNumElements() const { return NumElements; }\n411:   Type *getElementType() const { return ContainedType; }\n412: \n413:   /// This static method is the primary way to construct an ArrayType\n414:   LLVM_ABI static ArrayType *get(Type *ElementType, uint64_t NumElements);\n415: \n416:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a element type.\n417:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidElementType(Type *ElemTy);\n418: \n419:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n420:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n421:     return T->getTypeID() == ArrayTyID;\n422:   }\n423: };\n424: \n425: uint64_t Type::getArrayNumElements() const {\n426:   return cast<ArrayType>(this)->getNumElements();\n427: }\n428: \n429: /// Base class of all SIMD vector types\n430: class VectorType : public Type {\n431:   /// A fully specified VectorType is of the form <vscale x n x Ty>. 'n' is the\n432:   /// minimum number of elements of type Ty contained within the vector, and\n433:   /// 'vscale x' indicates that the total element count is an integer multiple\n434:   /// of 'n', where the multiple is either guaranteed to be one, or is\n435:   /// statically unknown at compile time.\n436:   ///\n437:   /// If the multiple is known to be 1, then the extra term is discarded in\n438:   /// textual IR:\n439:   ///\n440:   /// <4 x i32>          - a vector containing 4 i32s\n441:   /// <vscale x 4 x i32> - a vector containing an unknown integer multiple\n442:   ///                      of 4 i32s\n443: \n444:   /// The element type of the vector.\n445:   Type *ContainedType;\n446: \n447: protected:\n448:   /// The element quantity of this vector. The meaning of this value depends\n449:   /// on the type of vector:\n450:   /// - For FixedVectorType = <ElementQuantity x ty>, there are\n451:   ///   exactly ElementQuantity elements in this vector.\n452:   /// - For ScalableVectorType = <vscale x ElementQuantity x ty>,\n453:   ///   there are vscale * ElementQuantity elements in this vector, where\n454:   ///   vscale is a runtime-constant integer greater than 0.\n455:   const unsigned ElementQuantity;\n456: \n457:   LLVM_ABI VectorType(Type *ElType, unsigned EQ, Type::TypeID TID);\n458: \n459: public:\n460:   VectorType(const VectorType &) = delete;\n461:   VectorType &operator=(const VectorType &) = delete;\n462: \n463:   Type *getElementType() const { return ContainedType; }\n464: \n465:   /// This static method is the primary way to construct an VectorType.\n466:   LLVM_ABI static VectorType *get(Type *ElementType, ElementCount EC);\n467: \n468:   static VectorType *get(Type *ElementType, unsigned NumElements,\n469:                          bool Scalable) {\n470:     return VectorType::get(ElementType,\n471:                            ElementCount::get(NumElements, Scalable));\n472:   }\n473: \n474:   static VectorType *get(Type *ElementType, const VectorType *Other) {\n475:     return VectorType::get(ElementType, Other->getElementCount());\n476:   }\n477: \n478:   /// This static method gets a VectorType with the same number of elements as\n479:   /// the input type, and the element type is an integer type of the same width\n480:   /// as the input element type.\n481:   static VectorType *getInteger(VectorType *VTy) {\n482:     unsigned EltBits =\n483:         VTy->getElementType()->getPrimitiveSizeInBits().getFixedValue();\n484:     assert(EltBits && \"Element size must be of a non-zero size\");\n485:     Type *EltTy = IntegerType::get(VTy->getContext(), EltBits);\n486:     return VectorType::get(EltTy, VTy->getElementCount());\n487:   }\n488: \n489:   /// This static method is like getInteger except that the element types are\n490:   /// twice as wide as the elements in the input type.\n491:   static VectorType *getExtendedElementVectorType(VectorType *VTy) {\n492:     assert(VTy->isIntOrIntVectorTy() && \"VTy expected to be a vector of ints.\");\n493:     auto *EltTy = cast<IntegerType>(VTy->getElementType());\n494:     return VectorType::get(EltTy->getExtendedType(), VTy->getElementCount());\n495:   }\n496: \n497:   // This static method gets a VectorType with the same number of elements as\n498:   // the input type, and the element type is an integer or float type which\n499:   // is half as wide as the elements in the input type.\n500:   static VectorType *getTruncatedElementVectorType(VectorType *VTy) {\n501:     Type *EltTy;\n502:     if (VTy->getElementType()->isFloatingPointTy()) {\n503:       switch(VTy->getElementType()->getTypeID()) {\n504:       case DoubleTyID:\n505:         EltTy = Type::getFloatTy(VTy->getContext());\n506:         break;\n507:       case FloatTyID:\n508:         EltTy = Type::getHalfTy(VTy->getContext());\n509:         break;\n510:       default:\n511:         llvm_unreachable(\"Cannot create narrower fp vector element type\");\n512:       }\n513:     } else {\n514:       unsigned EltBits =\n515:           VTy->getElementType()->getPrimitiveSizeInBits().getFixedValue();\n516:       assert((EltBits & 1) == 0 &&\n517:              \"Cannot truncate vector element with odd bit-width\");\n518:       EltTy = IntegerType::get(VTy->getContext(), EltBits / 2);\n519:     }\n520:     return VectorType::get(EltTy, VTy->getElementCount());\n521:   }\n522: \n523:   // This static method returns a VectorType with a larger number of elements\n524:   // of a smaller type than the input element type. For example, a <4 x i64>\n525:   // subdivided twice would return <16 x i16>\n526:   static VectorType *getSubdividedVectorType(VectorType *VTy, int NumSubdivs) {\n527:     for (int i = 0; i < NumSubdivs; ++i) {\n528:       VTy = VectorType::getDoubleElementsVectorType(VTy);\n529:       VTy = VectorType::getTruncatedElementVectorType(VTy);\n530:     }\n531:     return VTy;\n532:   }\n533: \n534:   /// This static method returns a VectorType with half as many elements as the\n535:   /// input type and the same element type.\n536:   static VectorType *getHalfElementsVectorType(VectorType *VTy) {\n537:     auto EltCnt = VTy->getElementCount();\n538:     assert(EltCnt.isKnownEven() &&\n539:            \"Cannot halve vector with odd number of elements.\");\n540:     return VectorType::get(VTy->getElementType(),\n541:                            EltCnt.divideCoefficientBy(2));\n542:   }\n543: \n544:   static VectorType *getOneNthElementsVectorType(VectorType *VTy,\n545:                                                  unsigned Denominator) {\n546:     auto EltCnt = VTy->getElementCount();\n547:     assert(EltCnt.isKnownMultipleOf(Denominator) &&\n548:            \"Cannot take one-nth of a vector\");\n549:     return VectorType::get(VTy->getScalarType(),\n550:                            EltCnt.divideCoefficientBy(Denominator));\n551:   }\n552: \n553:   /// This static method returns a VectorType with twice as many elements as the\n554:   /// input type and the same element type.\n555:   static VectorType *getDoubleElementsVectorType(VectorType *VTy) {\n556:     auto EltCnt = VTy->getElementCount();\n557:     assert((EltCnt.getKnownMinValue() * 2ull) <= UINT_MAX &&\n558:            \"Too many elements in vector\");\n559:     return VectorType::get(VTy->getElementType(), EltCnt * 2);\n560:   }\n561: \n562:   /// This static method attempts to construct a VectorType with the same\n563:   /// size-in-bits as SizeTy but with an element type that matches the scalar\n564:   /// type of EltTy. The VectorType is returned on success, nullptr otherwise.\n565:   static VectorType *getWithSizeAndScalar(VectorType *SizeTy, Type *EltTy) {\n566:     if (SizeTy->getScalarType() == EltTy->getScalarType())\n567:       return SizeTy;\n568: \n569:     unsigned EltSize = EltTy->getScalarSizeInBits();\n570:     if (!SizeTy->getPrimitiveSizeInBits().isKnownMultipleOf(EltSize))\n571:       return nullptr;\n572: \n573:     ElementCount EC = SizeTy->getElementCount()\n574:                           .multiplyCoefficientBy(SizeTy->getScalarSizeInBits())\n575:                           .divideCoefficientBy(EltSize);\n576:     return VectorType::get(EltTy->getScalarType(), EC);\n577:   }\n578: \n579:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a element type.\n580:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidElementType(Type *ElemTy);\n581: \n582:   /// Return an ElementCount instance to represent the (possibly scalable)\n583:   /// number of elements in the vector.\n584:   inline ElementCount getElementCount() const;\n585: \n586:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n587:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n588:     return T->getTypeID() == FixedVectorTyID ||\n589:            T->getTypeID() == ScalableVectorTyID;\n590:   }\n591: };\n592: \n593: /// Class to represent fixed width SIMD vectors\n594: class FixedVectorType : public VectorType {\n595: protected:\n596:   FixedVectorType(Type *ElTy, unsigned NumElts)\n597:       : VectorType(ElTy, NumElts, FixedVectorTyID) {}\n598: \n599: public:\n600:   LLVM_ABI static FixedVectorType *get(Type *ElementType, unsigned NumElts);\n601: \n602:   static FixedVectorType *get(Type *ElementType, const FixedVectorType *FVTy) {\n603:     return get(ElementType, FVTy->getNumElements());\n604:   }\n605: \n606:   static FixedVectorType *getInteger(FixedVectorType *VTy) {\n607:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(VectorType::getInteger(VTy));\n608:   }\n609: \n610:   static FixedVectorType *getExtendedElementVectorType(FixedVectorType *VTy) {\n611:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(VectorType::getExtendedElementVectorType(VTy));\n612:   }\n613: \n614:   static FixedVectorType *getTruncatedElementVectorType(FixedVectorType *VTy) {\n615:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(\n616:         VectorType::getTruncatedElementVectorType(VTy));\n617:   }\n618: \n619:   static FixedVectorType *getSubdividedVectorType(FixedVectorType *VTy,\n620:                                                   int NumSubdivs) {\n621:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(\n622:         VectorType::getSubdividedVectorType(VTy, NumSubdivs));\n623:   }\n624: \n625:   static FixedVectorType *getHalfElementsVectorType(FixedVectorType *VTy) {\n626:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(VectorType::getHalfElementsVectorType(VTy));\n627:   }\n628: \n629:   static FixedVectorType *getDoubleElementsVectorType(FixedVectorType *VTy) {\n630:     return cast<FixedVectorType>(VectorType::getDoubleElementsVectorType(VTy));\n631:   }\n632: \n633:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n634:     return T->getTypeID() == FixedVectorTyID;\n635:   }\n636: \n637:   unsigned getNumElements() const { return ElementQuantity; }\n638: };\n639: \n640: /// Class to represent scalable SIMD vectors\n641: class ScalableVectorType : public VectorType {\n642: protected:\n643:   ScalableVectorType(Type *ElTy, unsigned MinNumElts)\n644:       : VectorType(ElTy, MinNumElts, ScalableVectorTyID) {}\n645: \n646: public:\n647:   LLVM_ABI static ScalableVectorType *get(Type *ElementType,\n648:                                           unsigned MinNumElts);\n649: \n650:   static ScalableVectorType *get(Type *ElementType,\n651:                                  const ScalableVectorType *SVTy) {\n652:     return get(ElementType, SVTy->getMinNumElements());\n653:   }\n654: \n655:   static ScalableVectorType *getInteger(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\n656:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(VectorType::getInteger(VTy));\n657:   }\n658: \n659:   static ScalableVectorType *\n660:   getExtendedElementVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\n661:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(\n662:         VectorType::getExtendedElementVectorType(VTy));\n663:   }\n664: \n665:   static ScalableVectorType *\n666:   getTruncatedElementVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\n667:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(\n668:         VectorType::getTruncatedElementVectorType(VTy));\n669:   }\n670: \n671:   static ScalableVectorType *getSubdividedVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy,\n672:                                                      int NumSubdivs) {\n673:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(\n674:         VectorType::getSubdividedVectorType(VTy, NumSubdivs));\n675:   }\n676: \n677:   static ScalableVectorType *\n678:   getHalfElementsVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\n679:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(VectorType::getHalfElementsVectorType(VTy));\n680:   }\n681: \n682:   static ScalableVectorType *\n683:   getDoubleElementsVectorType(ScalableVectorType *VTy) {\n684:     return cast<ScalableVectorType>(\n685:         VectorType::getDoubleElementsVectorType(VTy));\n686:   }\n687: \n688:   /// Get the minimum number of elements in this vector. The actual number of\n689:   /// elements in the vector is an integer multiple of this value.\n690:   unsigned getMinNumElements() const { return ElementQuantity; }\n691: \n692:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n693:     return T->getTypeID() == ScalableVectorTyID;\n694:   }\n695: };\n696: \n697: inline ElementCount VectorType::getElementCount() const {\n698:   return ElementCount::get(ElementQuantity, isa<ScalableVectorType>(this));\n699: }\n700: \n701: /// Class to represent pointers.\n702: class PointerType : public Type {\n703:   explicit PointerType(LLVMContext &C, unsigned AddrSpace);\n704: \n705: public:\n706:   PointerType(const PointerType &) = delete;\n707:   PointerType &operator=(const PointerType &) = delete;\n708: \n709:   /// This constructs a pointer to an object of the specified type in a numbered\n710:   /// address space.\n711:   [[deprecated(\"PointerType::get with pointee type is pending removal. Use \"\n712:                \"Context overload.\")]]\n713:   LLVM_ABI static PointerType *get(Type *ElementType, unsigned AddressSpace);\n714:   /// This constructs an opaque pointer to an object in a numbered address\n715:   /// space.\n716:   LLVM_ABI static PointerType *get(LLVMContext &C, unsigned AddressSpace);\n717: \n718:   /// This constructs a pointer to an object of the specified type in the\n719:   /// default address space (address space zero).\n720:   [[deprecated(\"PointerType::getUnqual with pointee type is pending removal. \"\n721:                \"Use Context overload.\")]]\n722:   static PointerType *getUnqual(Type *ElementType) {\n723:     assert(ElementType && \"Can't get a pointer to <null> type!\");\n724:     assert(isValidElementType(ElementType) &&\n725:            \"Invalid type for pointer element!\");\n726:     return PointerType::getUnqual(ElementType->getContext());\n727:   }\n728: \n729:   /// This constructs an opaque pointer to an object in the\n730:   /// default address space (address space zero).\n731:   static PointerType *getUnqual(LLVMContext &C) {\n732:     return PointerType::get(C, 0);\n733:   }\n734: \n735:   /// Return true if the specified type is valid as a element type.\n736:   LLVM_ABI static bool isValidElementType(Type *ElemTy);\n737: \n738:   /// Return true if we can load or store from a pointer to this type.\n739:   LLVM_ABI static bool isLoadableOrStorableType(Type *ElemTy);\n740: \n741:   /// Return the address space of the Pointer type.\n742:   inline unsigned getAddressSpace() const { return getSubclassData(); }\n743: \n744:   /// Implement support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n745:   static bool classof(const Type *T) {\n746:     return T->getTypeID() == PointerTyID;\n747:   }\n748: };\n749: \n750: Type *Type::getExtendedType() const {\n751:   assert(\n752:       isIntOrIntVectorTy() &&\n753:       \"Original type expected to be a vector of integers or a scalar integer.\");\n754:   if (auto *VTy = dyn_cast<VectorType>(this))\n755:     return VectorType::getExtendedElementVectorType(\n756:         const_cast<VectorType *>(VTy));\n757:   return cast<IntegerType>(this)->getExtendedType();\n758: }\n759: \n760: Type *Type::getWithNewType(Type *EltTy) const {\n761:   if (auto *VTy = dyn_cast<VectorType>(this))\n762:     return VectorType::get(EltTy, VTy->getElementCount());\n763:   return EltTy;\n764: }\n765: \n766: Type *Type::getWithNewBitWidth(unsigned NewBitWidth) const {\n767:   assert(\n768:       isIntOrIntVectorTy() &&\n769:       \"Original type expected to be a vector of integers or a scalar integer.\");\n770:   return getWithNewType(getIntNTy(getContext(), NewBitWidth));\n771: }\n772: \n773: unsigned Type::getPointerAddressSpace() const {\n774:   return cast<PointerType>(getScalarType())->getAddressSpace();\n775: }\n776: \n777: /// Class to represent target extensions types, which are generally\n778: /// unintrospectable from target-independent optimizations.\n779: ///\n780: /// Target extension types have a string name, and optionally have type and/or\n781: /// integer parameters. The exact meaning of any parameters is dependent on the\n782: /// target.\n783: class TargetExtType : public Type {\n784:   TargetExtType(LLVMContext &C, StringRef Name, ArrayRef<Type *> Types,\n785:                 ArrayRef<unsigned> Ints);\n786: \n787:   // These strings are ultimately owned by the context.\n788:   StringRef Name;\n789:   unsigned *IntParams;\n790: \n791: public:\n792:   TargetExtType(const TargetExtType &) = delete;\n793:   TargetExtType &operator=(const TargetExtType &) = delete;\n794: \n795:   /// Return a target extension type having the specified name and optional\n796:   /// type and integer parameters.\n797:   LLVM_ABI static TargetExtType *get(LLVMContext &Context, StringRef Name,\n798:                                      ArrayRef<Type *> Types = {},\n799:                                      ArrayRef<unsigned> Ints = {});\n800: \n801:   /// Return a target extension type having the specified name and optional\n802:   /// type and integer parameters, or an appropriate Error if it fails the\n803:   /// parameters check.\n804:   LLVM_ABI static Expected<TargetExtType *>\n805:   getOrError(LLVMContext &Context, StringRef Name, ArrayRef<Type *> Types = {},\n806:              ArrayRef<unsigned> Ints = {});\n807: \n808:   /// Check that a newly created target extension type has the expected number\n809:   /// of type parameters and integer parameters, returning the type itself if OK\n810:   /// or an appropriate Error if not.\n811:   LLVM_ABI static Expected<TargetExtType *> checkParams(TargetExtType *TTy);\n812: \n813:   /// Return the name for this target extension type. Two distinct target\n814:   /// extension types may have the same name if their type or integer parameters\n815:   /// differ.\n816:   StringRef getName() const { return Name; }\n817: \n818:   /// Return the type parameters for this particular target extension type. If\n819:   /// there are no parameters, an empty array is returned.\n820:   ArrayRef<Type *> type_params() const {\n821:     return ArrayRef(type_param_begin(), type_param_end());\n822:   }\n823: \n824:   using type_param_iterator = Type::subtype_iterator;\n825:   type_param_iterator type_param_begin() const { return ContainedTys; }\n826:   type_param_iterator type_param_end() const {\n827:     return &ContainedTys[NumContainedTys];\n828:   }\n829: \n830:   Type *getTypeParameter(unsigned i) const { return getContainedType(i); }\n831:   unsigned getNumTypeParameters() const { return getNumContainedTypes(); }\n832: \n833:   /// Return the integer parameters for this particular target extension type.\n834:   /// If there are no parameters, an empty array is returned.\n835:   ArrayRef<unsigned> int_params() const {\n836:     return ArrayRef(IntParams, getNumIntParameters());\n837:   }\n838: \n839:   unsigned getIntParameter(unsigned i) const { return IntParams[i]; }\n840:   unsigned getNumIntParameters() const { return getSubclassData(); }\n841: \n842:   enum Property {\n843:     /// zeroinitializer is valid for this target extension type.\n844:     HasZeroInit = 1U << 0,\n845:     /// This type may be used as the value type of a global variable.\n846:     CanBeGlobal = 1U << 1,\n847:     /// This type may be allocated on the stack, either as the allocated type\n848:     /// of an alloca instruction or as a byval function parameter.\n849:     CanBeLocal = 1U << 2,\n850:     /// This type may be used as an element in a vector.\n851:     CanBeVectorElement = 1U << 3,\n852:     // This type can only be used in intrinsic arguments and return values.\n853:     /// In particular, it cannot be used in select and phi instructions.\n854:     IsTokenLike = 1U << 4,\n855:   };\n856: \n857:   /// Returns true if the target extension type contains the given property.\n858:   LLVM_ABI bool hasProperty(Property Prop) const;\n859: \n860:   /// Returns an underlying layout type for the target extension type. This\n861:   /// type can be used to query size and alignment information, if it is\n862:   /// appropriate (although note that the layout type may also be void). It is\n863:   /// not legal to bitcast between this type and the layout type, however.\n864:   LLVM_ABI Type *getLayoutType() const;\n865: \n866:   /// Methods for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.\n867:   static bool classof(const Type *T) { return T->getTypeID() == TargetExtTyID; }\n868: };\n869: \n870: StringRef Type::getTargetExtName() const {\n871:   return cast<TargetExtType>(this)->getName();\n872: }\n873: \n874: } // end namespace llvm\n875: \n876: #endif // LLVM_IR_DERIVEDTYPES_H\n877: ", "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h"}, "isError": false}

IsVarArgs)\n350:   : Type(Result->getContext(), FunctionTyID) {\n351:   Type **SubTys = reinterpret_cast<Type**>(this+1);\n352:   assert(isValidReturnType(Result) && \"invalid return type for function\");\n353:   setSubclassData(IsVarArgs);\n354: \n355:   SubTys[0] = Result;\n356: \n357:   for (unsigned i = 0, e = Params.size(); i != e; ++i) {\n358: *ReturnType,\n369:                                 ArrayRef<Type*> Params, bool isVarArg) {\n370:   LLVMContextImpl *pImpl = ReturnType->getContext().pImpl;\n371:   const FunctionTypeKeyInfo::KeyTy Key(ReturnType, Params, isVarArg);\n372:   FunctionType *FT;\n373:   // Since we only want to allocate a fresh function type in case none is found\n374:   // and we don't want to perform two lookups (one for checking if existent and\n375:   // one for inserting the newly allocated one), here we instead lookup based on\n376:   // Key and update the reference to the function type in-place to a newly\n377:   // allocated one if not found.\n378:   auto Insertion = pImpl->FunctionTypes.insert_as(nullptr, Key);\n379:   if (Insertion.second) {\n380:     // The function type was not found. Allocate one and update FunctionTypes\n381:     // in-place.\n382:     FT = (FunctionType *)pImpl->Alloc.Allocate(\n383:         sizeof(FunctionType) + sizeof(Type *) * (Params.size() + 1),\n384:         alignof(FunctionType));\n385:     new (FT) FunctionType(ReturnType, Params, isVarArg);\n386:     *Insertion.first = FT;\n387:   } else {\n388:     // The function type was found. ArgTys>\n972:   TargetTypeInfo(Type *LayoutType, ArgTys... Properties)\n973:       : LayoutType(LayoutType), Properties((0 | ... | Properties)) {\n974:     assert((!(this->Properties & TargetExtType::CanBeVectorElement) ||\n975:             LayoutType->isSized()) &&\n976:            \"Vector element type must be sized\");\n977:   }\n978: };\n979: } // anonymous namespace\n980: \n981: static TargetTypeInfo getTargetTypeInfo(const TargetExtType *Ty) {\n982:   LLVMContext &C = Ty->getContext();\n983:   StringRef Name = Ty->getName();\n984:   if (Name == \"spirv.Image\" || Name == \"spirv.SignedImage\")\n985:     return TargetTypeInfo(PointerType::get(C, 0), TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal,\n986:                           TargetExtType::CanBeLocal);\n987:   if (Name == \"spirv.Type\") {\n988:     assert(Ty->getNumIntParameters() == 3 &&\n989:            \"Wrong number of parameters for spirv.Type\");\n990: \n991:     auto Size = Ty->getIntParameter(1);\n992:     auto Alignment = Ty->getIntParameter(2);\n993: \n994:     llvm::Type *LayoutType = nullptr;\n995:     if (Size > 0 && Alignment > 0) {\n996:       LayoutType =\n997:           ArrayType::get(Type::getIntNTy(C, Alignment), Size * 8 / Alignment);\n998:     } else {\n999:       // LLVM expects variables that can be allocated to have an alignment and\n1000:       // size. Default to using a 32-bit int as the layout type if none are\n1001:       // present.\n1002:       LayoutType = Type::getInt32Ty(C);\n1003:     }\n1004: \n1005:     return TargetTypeInfo(LayoutType, TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal,\n1006:                           TargetExtType::CanBeLocal);\n1007:   }\n1008:   if (Name == \"spirv.IntegralConstant\" || Name == \"spirv.Literal\")\n1009:     return TargetTypeInfo(Type::getVoidTy(C));\n1010:   if (Name == \"spirv.Padding\")\n1011:     return TargetTypeInfo(\n1012:         ArrayType::get(Type::getInt8Ty(C), Ty->getIntParameter(0)),\n1013:         TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal);\n1014:   if (Name.starts_with(\"spirv.\"))\n1015:     return TargetTypeInfo(PointerType::get(C, 0), TargetExtType::HasZeroInit,\n1016:                           TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal,\n1017:                           TargetExtType::CanBeLocal);\n1018: \n1019:   // Opaque types in the AArch64 name space.\n1020:   if (Name == \"aarch64.svcount\")\n1021:     return TargetTypeInfo(ScalableVectorType::get(Type::getInt1Ty(C), 16),\n1022:                           TargetExtType::HasZeroInit,\n1023:                           TargetExtType::CanBeLocal);\n1024: \n1025:   // RISC-V vector tuple type. The layout is represented as the type that needs\n1026:   // the same number of vector registers(VREGS) as this tuple type, represented\n1027:   // as <vscale x (RVVBitsPerBlock * VREGS / 8) x i8>.\n1028:   if (Name == \"riscv.vector.tuple\") {\n1029:     unsigned TotalNumElts =\n1030:         std::max(cast<ScalableVectorType>(Ty->getTypeParameter(0))\n1031:                      ->getMinNumElements(),\n1032:                  RISCV::RVVBytesPerBlock) *\n1033:         Ty->getIntParameter(0);\n1034:     return TargetTypeInfo(\n1035:         ScalableVectorType::get(Type::getInt8Ty(C), TotalNumElts),\n1036:         TargetExtType::CanBeLocal, TargetExtType::HasZeroInit);\n1037:   }\n1038: \n1039:   // DirectX resources\n1040:   if (Name == \"dx.Padding\")\n1041:     return TargetTypeInfo(\n1042:         ArrayType::get(Type::getInt8Ty(C), Ty->getIntParameter(0)),\n1043:         TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal);\n1044:   if (Name.starts_with(\"dx.\"))\n1045:     return TargetTypeInfo(PointerType::get(C, 0), TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal,\n1046:                           TargetExtType::CanBeLocal,\n1047:                           TargetExtType::IsTokenLike);\n1048: \n1049:   // Opaque types in the AMDGPU name space.\n1050:   if (Name == \"amdgcn.named.barrier\") {\n1051:     return TargetTypeInfo(FixedVectorType::get(Type::getInt32Ty(C), 4),\n1052:                           TargetExtType::CanBeGlobal);\n1053:   }\n1054: \n1055:   // Type used to test vector element target extension property.\n1056:   // Can be removed once a public target extension type uses CanBeVectorElement.\n1057:   if (Name == \"llvm.test.vectorelement\") {\n1058:     return TargetTypeInfo(Type::getInt32Ty(C), TargetExtType::CanBeLocal,\n1059:                           TargetExtType::CanBeVectorElement);\n1060:   }\n1061: \n1062:   return TargetTypeInfo(Type::getVoidTy(C));\n1063: }\n1064: \n1065: bool Type::isTokenLikeTy() const {\n1066:   if (isTokenTy())\n1067:     return true;\n1068:   if (auto *TT = dyn_cast<TargetExtType>(this))\n1069:     return TT->hasProperty(TargetExtType::Property::IsTokenLike);\n1070:   return false;\n1071: }\n1072: \n1073: Type *TargetExtType::getLayoutType() const {\n1074:   return getTargetTypeInfo(this).LayoutType;\n1075: }\n1076: \n1077: bool TargetExtType::hasProperty(Property Prop) const {\n1078:   uint64_t Properties = getTargetTypeInfo(this).Properties;\n1079:   return (Properties & Prop) == Prop;\n1080: }\n1081: ", "url": {"meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\"content\":\"1: //===- llvm/LLVMContext.h - Class for managing \\\"global\\\" state ---*- C++ -*-===//\\n2: //\\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\\n6: //\\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\\n8: //\\n9: // This file declares LLVMContext, a container of \\\"global\\\" state in LLVM, such\\n10: // as the global type and constant uniquing tables.\\n11: //\\n12: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\\n13: \\n14: #ifndef LLVM_IR_LLVMCONTEXT_H\\n15: #define LLVM_IR_LLVMCONTEXT_H\\n16: \\n17: #include \\\"llvm-c/Types.h\\\"\\n18: #include \\\"llvm/IR/DiagnosticHandler.h\\\"\\n19: #include \\\"llvm/Support/CBindingWrapping.h\\\"\\n20: #include \\\"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\\\"\\n21: #include \\u003ccstdint\\u003e\\n22: #include \\u003cmemory\\u003e\\n23: #include \\u003coptional\\u003e\\n24: #include \\u003cstring\\u003e\\n25: \\n26: namespace llvm {\\n27: \\n28: class DiagnosticInfo;\\n29: enum DiagnosticSeverity : char;\\n30: class Function;\\n31: class Instruction;\\n32: class LLVMContextImpl;\\n33: class Module;\\n34: class OptPassGate;\\n35: template \\u003ctypename T\\u003e class SmallVectorImpl;\\n36: template \\u003ctypename T\\u003e class StringMapEntry;\\n37: class StringRef;\\n38: class Twine;\\n39: class LLVMRemarkStreamer;\\n40: \\n41: namespace remarks {\\n42: class RemarkStreamer;\\n43: }\\n44: \\n45: namespace SyncScope {\\n46: \\n47: typedef uint8_t ID;\\n48: \\n49: /// Known synchronization scope IDs, which always have the same value.  All\\n50: /// synchronization scope IDs that LLVM has special knowledge of are listed\\n51: /// here.  Additionally, this scheme allows LLVM to efficiently check for\\n52: /// specific synchronization scope ID without comparing strings.\\n53: enum {\\n54:   /// Synchronized with respect to signal handlers executing in the same thread.\\n55:   SingleThread = 0,\\n56: \\n57:   /// Synchronized with respect to all concurrently executing threads.\\n58:   System = 1\\n59: };\\n60: \\n61: } // end namespace SyncScope\\n62: \\n63: /// This is an important class for using LLVM in a threaded context.  It\\n64: /// (opaquely) owns and manages the core \\\"global\\\" data of LLVM's core\\n65: /// infrastructure, including the type and constant uniquing tables.\\n66: /// LLVMContext itself provides no locking guarantees, so you should be careful\\n67: /// to have one context per thread.\\n68: class LLVMContext {\\n69: public:\\n70:   LLVMContextImpl *const pImpl;\\n71:   LLVM_ABI LLVMContext();\\n72:   LLVMContext(const LLVMContext \\u0026) = delete;\\n73:   LLVMContext \\u0026operator=(const LLVMContext \\u0026) = delete;\\n74:   LLVM_ABI ~LLVMContext();\\n75: \\n76:   // Pinned metadata names, which always have the same value.  This is a\\n77:   // compile-time performance optimization, not a correctness optimization.\\n78:   enum : unsigned {\\n79: #define LLVM_FIXED_MD_KIND(EnumID, Name, Value) EnumID = Value,\\n80: #include \\\"llvm/IR/FixedMetadataKinds.def\\\"\\n81: #undef LLVM_FIXED_MD_KIND\\n82:   };\\n83: \\n84:   /// Known operand bundle tag IDs, which always have the same value.  All\\n85:   /// operand bundle tags that LLVM has special knowledge of are listed here.\\n86:   /// Additionally, this scheme allows LLVM to efficiently check for specific\\n87:   /// operand bundle tags without comparing strings. Keep this in sync with\\n88:   /// LLVMContext::LLVMContext().\\n89:   enum : unsigned {\\n90:     OB_deopt = 0,                  // \\\"deopt\\\"\\n91:     OB_funclet = 1,                // \\\"funclet\\\"\\n92:     OB_gc_transition = 2,          // \\\"gc-transition\\\"\\n93:     OB_cfguardtarget = 3,          // \\\"cfguardtarget\\\"\\n94:     OB_preallocated = 4,           // \\\"preallocated\\\"\\n95:     OB_gc_live = 5,                // \\\"gc-live\\\"\\n96:     OB_clang_arc_attachedcall = 6, // \\\"clang.arc.attachedcall\\\"\\n97:     OB_ptrauth = 7,                // \\\"ptrauth\\\"\\n98:     OB_kcfi = 8,                   // \\\"kcfi\\\"\\n99:     OB_convergencectrl = 9,        // \\\"convergencectrl\\\"\\n100:     OB_align = 10,                 // \\\"align\\\"\\n101:     OB_deactivation_symbol = 11,   // \\\"deactivation-symbol\\\"\\n102:     OB_LastBundleID = OB_deactivation_symbol\\n103:   };\\n104: \\n105:   /// getMDKindID - Return a unique non-zero ID for the specified metadata kind.\\n106:   /// This ID is uniqued across modules in the current LLVMContext.\\n107:   LLVM_ABI unsigned getMDKindID(StringRef Name) const;\\n108: \\n109:   /// getMDKindNames - Populate client supplied SmallVector with the name for\\n110:   /// custom metadata IDs registered in this LLVMContext.\\n111:   LLVM_ABI void getMDKindNames(SmallVectorImpl\\u003cStringRef\\u003e \\u0026Result) const;\\n112: \\n113:   /// getOperandBundleTags - Populate client supplied SmallVector with the\\n114:   /// bundle tags registered in this LLVMContext.  The bundle tags are ordered\\n115:   /// by increasing bundle IDs.\\n116:   /// \\\\see LLVMContext::getOperandBundleTagID\\n117:   LLVM_ABI void getOperandBundleTags(SmallVectorImpl\\u003cStringRef\\u003e \\u0026Result) const;\\n118: \\n119:   /// getOrInsertBundleTag - Returns the Tag to use for an operand bundle of\\n120:   /// name TagName.\\n121:   LLVM_ABI StringMapEntry\\u003cuint32_t\\u003e *\\n122:   getOrInsertBundleTag(StringRef TagName) const;\\n123: \\n124:   /// getOperandBundleTagID - Maps a bundle tag to an integer ID.  Every bundle\\n125:   /// tag registered with an LLVMContext has an unique ID.\\n126:   LLVM_ABI uint32_t getOperandBundleTagID(StringRef Tag) const;\\n127: \\n128:   /// getOrInsertSyncScopeID - Maps synchronization scope name to\\n129:   /// synchronization scope ID.  Every synchronization scope registered with\\n130:   /// LLVMContext has unique ID except pre-defined ones.\\n131:   LLVM_ABI SyncScope::ID getOrInsertSyncScopeID(StringRef SSN);\\n132: \\n133:   /// getSyncScopeNames - Populates client supplied SmallVector with\\n134:   /// synchronization scope names registered with LLVMContext.  Synchronization\\n135:   /// scope names are ordered by increasing synchronization scope IDs.\\n136:   LLVM_ABI void getSyncScopeNames(SmallVectorImpl\\u003cStringRef\\u003e \\u0026SSNs) const;\\n137: \\n138:   /// getSyncScopeName - Returns the name of a SyncScope::ID\\n139:   /// registered with LLVMContext, if any.\\n140:   LLVM_ABI std::optional\\u003cStringRef\\u003e getSyncScopeName(SyncScope::ID Id) const;\\n141: \\n142:   /// Define the GC for a function\\n143:   LLVM_ABI void setGC(const Function \\u0026Fn, std::string GCName);\\n144: \\n145:   /// Return the GC for a function\\n146:   LLVM_ABI const std::string \\u0026getGC(const Function \\u0026Fn);\\n147: \\n148:   /// Remove the GC for a function\\n149:   LLVM_ABI void deleteGC(const Function \\u0026Fn);\\n150: \\n151:   /// Return true if the Context runtime configuration is set to discard all\\n152:   /// value names. When true, only GlobalValue names will be available in the\\n153:   /// IR.\\n154:   LLVM_ABI bool shouldDiscardValueNames() const;\\n155: \\n156:   /// Set the Context runtime configuration to discard all value name (but\\n157:   /// GlobalValue). Clients can use this flag to save memory and runtime,\\n158:   /// especially in release mode.\\n159:   LLVM_ABI void setDiscardValueNames(bool Discard);\\n160: \\n161:   /// Whether there is a string map for uniquing debug info\\n162:   /// identifiers across the context.  Off by default.\\n163:   LLVM_ABI bool isODRUniquingDebugTypes() const;\\n164:   LLVM_ABI void enableDebugTypeODRUniquing();\\n165:   LLVM_ABI void disableDebugTypeODRUniquing();\\n166: \\n167:   /// generateMachineFunctionNum - Get a unique number for MachineFunction\\n168:   /// that associated with the given Function.\\n169:   LLVM_ABI unsigned generateMachineFunctionNum(Function \\u0026);\\n170: \\n171:   /// Defines the type of a yield callback.\\n172:   /// \\\\see LLVMContext::setYieldCallback.\\n173:   using YieldCallbackTy = void (*)(LLVMContext *Context, void *OpaqueHandle);\\n174: \\n175:   /// setDiagnosticHandlerCallBack - This method sets a handler call back\\n176:   /// that is invoked when the backend needs to report anything to the user.\\n177:   /// The first argument is a function pointer and the second is a context pointer\\n178:   /// that gets passed into the DiagHandler.  The third argument should be set to\\n179:   /// true if the handler only expects enabled diagnostics.\\n180:   ///\\n181:   /// LLVMContext doesn't take ownership or interpret either of these\\n182:   /// pointers.\\n183:   LLVM_ABI void setDiagnosticHandlerCallBack(\\n184:       DiagnosticHandler::DiagnosticHandlerTy DiagHandler,\\n185:       void *DiagContext = nullptr, bool RespectFilters = false);\\n186: \\n187:   /// setDiagnosticHandler - This method sets unique_ptr to object of\\n188:   /// DiagnosticHandler to provide custom diagnostic handling. The first\\n189:   /// argument is unique_ptr of object of type DiagnosticHandler or a derived\\n190:   /// of that. The second argument should be set to true if the handler only\\n191:   /// expects enabled diagnostics.\\n192:   ///\\n193:   /// Ownership of this pointer is moved to LLVMContextImpl.\\n194:   LLVM_ABI void setDiagnosticHandler(std::unique_ptr\\u003cDiagnosticHandler\\u003e \\u0026\\u0026DH,\\n195:                                      bool RespectFilters = false);\\n196: \\n197:   /// getDiagnosticHandlerCallBack - Return the diagnostic handler call back set by\\n198:   /// setDiagnosticHandlerCallBack.\\n199:   LLVM_ABI DiagnosticHandler::DiagnosticHandlerTy\\n200:   getDiagnosticHandlerCallBack() const;\\n201: \\n202:   /// getDiagnosticContext - Return the diagnostic context set by\\n203:   /// setDiagnosticContext.\\n204:   LLVM_ABI void *getDiagnosticContext() const;\\n205: \\n206:   /// getDiagHandlerPtr - Returns const raw pointer of DiagnosticHandler set by\\n207:   /// setDiagnosticHandler.\\n208:   LLVM_ABI const DiagnosticHandler *getDiagHandlerPtr() const;\\n209: \\n210:   /// getDiagnosticHandler - transfers ownership of DiagnosticHandler unique_ptr\\n211:   /// to caller.\\n212:   LLVM_ABI std::unique_ptr\\u003cDiagnosticHandler\\u003e getDiagnosticHandler();\\n213: \\n214:   /// Return if a code hotness metric should be included in optimization\\n215:   /// diagnostics.\\n216:   LLVM_ABI bool getDiagnosticsHotnessRequested() const;\\n217:   /// Set if a code hotness metric should be included in optimization\\n218:   /// diagnostics.\\n219:   LLVM_ABI void setDiagnosticsHotnessRequested(bool Requested);\\n220: \\n221:   LLVM_ABI bool getMisExpectWarningRequested() const;\\n222:   LLVM_ABI void setMisExpectWarningRequested(bool Requested);\\n223:   LLVM_ABI void\\n224:   setDiagnosticsMisExpectTolerance(std::optional\\u003cuint32_t\\u003e Tolerance);\\n225:   LLVM_ABI uint32_t getDiagnosticsMisExpectTolerance() const;\\n226: \\n227:   /// Return the minimum hotness value a diagnostic would need in order\\n228:   /// to be included in optimization diagnostics.\\n229:   ///\\n230:   /// Three possible return values:\\n231:   /// 0            - threshold is disabled. Everything will be printed out.\\n232:   /// positive int - threshold is set.\\n233:   /// UINT64_MAX   - threshold is not yet set, and needs to be synced from\\n234:   ///                profile summary. Note that in case of missing profile\\n235:   ///                summary, threshold will be kept at \\\"MAX\\\", effectively\\n236:   ///                suppresses all remarks output.\\n237:   LLVM_ABI uint64_t getDiagnosticsHotnessThreshold() const;\\n238: \\n239:   /// Set the minimum hotness value a diagnostic needs in order to be\\n240:   /// included in optimization diagnostics.\\n241:   LLVM_ABI void\\n242:   setDiagnosticsHotnessThreshold(std::optional\\u003cuint64_t\\u003e Threshold);\\n243: \\n244:   /// Return if hotness threshold is requested from PSI.\\n245:   LLVM_ABI bool isDiagnosticsHotnessThresholdSetFromPSI() const;\\n246: \\n247:   /// The \\\"main remark streamer\\\" used by all the specialized remark streamers.\\n248:   /// This streamer keeps generic remark metadata in memory throughout the life\\n249:   /// of the LLVMContext. This metadata may be emitted in a section in object\\n250:   /// files depending on the format requirements.\\n251:   ///\\n252:   /// All specialized remark streamers should convert remarks to\\n253:   /// llvm::remarks::Remark and emit them through this streamer.\\n254:   LLVM_ABI remarks::RemarkStreamer *getMainRemarkStreamer();\\n255:   LLVM_ABI const remarks::RemarkStreamer *getMainRemarkStreamer() const;\\n256:   LLVM_ABI void setMainRemarkStreamer(\\n257:       std::unique_ptr\\u003cremarks::RemarkStreamer\\u003e MainRemarkStreamer);\\n258: \\n259:   /// The \\\"LLVM remark streamer\\\" used by LLVM to serialize remark diagnostics\\n260:   /// comming from IR and MIR passes.\\n261:   ///\\n262:   /// If it does not exist, diagnostics are not saved in a file but only emitted\\n263:   /// via the diagnostic handler.\\n264:   LLVM_ABI LLVMRemarkStreamer *getLLVMRemarkStreamer();\\n265:   LLVM_ABI const LLVMRemarkStreamer *getLLVMRemarkStreamer() const;\\n266:   LLVM_ABI void\\n267:   setLLVMRemarkStreamer(std::unique_ptr\\u003cLLVMRemarkStreamer\\u003e RemarkStreamer);\\n268: \\n269:   /// Get the prefix that should be printed in front of a diagnostic of\\n270:   ///        the given \\\\p Severity\\n271:   LLVM_ABI static const char *\\n272:   getDiagnosticMessagePrefix(DiagnosticSeverity Severity);\\n273: \\n274:   /// Report a message to the currently installed diagnostic handler.\\n275:   ///\\n276:   /// This function returns, in particular in the case of error reporting\\n277:   /// (DI.Severity == \\\\a DS_Error), so the caller should leave the compilation\\n278:   /// process in a self-consistent state, even though the generated code\\n279:   /// need not be correct.\\n280:   ///\\n281:   /// The diagnostic message will be implicitly prefixed with a severity keyword\\n282:   /// according to \\\\p DI.getSeverity(), i.e., \\\"error: \\\" for \\\\a DS_Error,\\n283:   /// \\\"warning: \\\" for \\\\a DS_Warning, and   "text": "## LLVM IR Type System to Code Generation Chain

### 1. Type Class Hierarchy (llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h, llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h)

The root of the type system is the `Type` class defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h`. It is an immutable, uniqued class that holds a `TypeID` enum (e.g., `IntegerTyID`, `FunctionTyID`, `StructTyID`, `PointerTyID`, `ArrayTyID`, `FixedVectorTyID`, `ScalableVectorTyID`, `TargetExtTyID`, etc.) and a back-reference to the owning `LLVMContext`. Types are never freed once created.

Derived types are declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h` and implemented in `llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp`:
- **`IntegerType`**: Represents arbitrary-width integers. Created via `IntegerType::get(LLVMContext &C, unsigned NumBits)`. Stores bit-width in the `SubclassData` field.
- **`FunctionType`**: Represents function signatures (return type + parameter types + vararg flag). Created via `FunctionType::get(Type *Result, ArrayRef<Type*> Params, bool isVarArg)`.
- **`StructType`**: Represents struct types. Literal structs (anonymous, uniqued structurally) are created via `StructType::get(LLVMContext &Context, ArrayRef<Type*> Elements, bool isPacked)`. Identified structs (named, not uniqued) are created via `StructType::create(LLVMContext &Context, StringRef Name)`.
- **`ArrayType`**, **`VectorType`** (`FixedVectorType`, `ScalableVectorType`), **`PointerType`**, **`TargetExtType`**: Other derived types.

### 2. Context Ownership (llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h, llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.h, llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.cpp)

All type instances are owned by `LLVMContext` (via its opaque implementation `LLVMContextImpl` in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h`). The context holds:
- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — uniquing table for integer types by bit-width.
- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — a `DenseSet<FunctionType *, FunctionTypeKeyInfo>` for uniquing function types by (return type, params, vararg).
- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — a `DenseSet<StructType *, AnonStructTypeKeyInfo>` for uniquing literal struct types.
- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — for identified (named) struct types.
- `DenseMap<std::pair<Type *, uint64_t>, ArrayType *> ArrayTypes`, `DenseMap<std::pair<Type *, ElementCount>, VectorType *> VectorTypes`, `DenseMap<unsigned, PointerType *> PointerTypes`, `TargetExtTypeSet TargetExtTypes`.
- Pre-allocated singleton instances for primitive types: `Type VoidTy, LabelTy, HalfTy, BFloatTy, FloatTy, DoubleTy, ...` and `IntegerType Int1Ty, Int8Ty, Int16Ty, Int32Ty, Int64Ty, Int128Ty`.
- A `BumpPtrAllocator Alloc` used to allocate all type objects.

The `LLVMContextImpl` also defines the key-info structs (`AnonStructTypeKeyInfo`, `FunctionTypeKeyInfo`, `TargetExtTypeKeyInfo`) used for hashing and equality in the uniquing sets.

Type factory methods (e.g., `IntegerType::get`, `FunctionType::get`, `StructType::get`) look up or insert into these tables via `C.pImpl->IntegerTypes`, `C.pImpl->FunctionTypes`, etc., ensuring that only one instance of each structurally equivalent type exists per context.

### 3. IR Type to Machine Type Lowering in SelectionDAG

The lowering chain from LLVM IR types to SelectionDAG machine types proceeds through several layers:

**Step 1: `EVT::getEVT(Type *Ty)` — `llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp`**
This function converts an LLVM IR `Type *` to an `EVT` (Extended Value Type, defined in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h`). It switches on `Ty->getTypeID()`: integers become `getIntegerVT(...)`, vectors become `getVectorVT(...)`, and other types fall through to `MVT::getVT(Ty, HandleUnknown)`.

**Step 2: `TargetLowering::getValueType(const DataLayout &DL, Type *Ty)` — `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h`**
This method wraps `EVT::getEVT` with pointer-type awareness: pointer types are lowered to the target's native pointer EVT via `getPointerTy(DL, AddrSpace)`, and vector-of-pointer types are handled similarly. This is the primary API used by code generation passes to convert IR types to machine types.

**Step 3: `ComputeValueTypes` and `ComputeValueVTs` — `llvm/lib/CodeGen/Analysis.cpp` (declared in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/Analysis.h`)**
`ComputeValueTypes` recursively decomposes aggregate IR types (structs, arrays) into a flat list of scalar `Type *` values. `ComputeValueVTs` then calls `TLI.getValueType(DL, Ty)` on each scalar type to produce a flat list of `EVT`s. This is the bridge that handles struct and array decomposition for code generation.

**Step 4: `SelectionDAGBuilder` — `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp`**
The `SelectionDAGBuilder` translates LLVM IR instructions into SelectionDAG nodes. For example:
- `visitLoad(const LoadInst &I)` calls `ComputeValueVTs(TLI, DAG.getDataLayout(), Ty, ValueVTs, &MemVTs, &Offsets)` to get the EVTs for the loaded type, then creates `ISD::LOAD` nodes for each component.
- `visitStore(const StoreInst &I)` similarly decomposes the stored type.
- Many other visitors call `TLI.getValueType(DAG.getDataLayout(), I.getType())` directly for scalar types.
- Function argument lowering (in `lowerFormalArguments`) calls `ComputeValueTypes` and then `TLI->getValueType(DL, ArgTy)` for each argument type.

**Step 5: `SelectionDAGISel` — `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGISel.cpp`**
The instruction selector operates on the SelectionDAG nodes (which carry EVT/MVT type information) and selects target machine instructions.

### Summary of the Chain

```
Type creation API (IntegerType::get, FunctionType::get, StructType::get, etc.)
  [llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h, llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp]
  ↓
Context ownership & uniquing (LLVMContextImpl: IntegerTypes, FunctionTypes, AnonStructTypes, NamedStructTypes)
  [llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h, llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.cpp]
  ↓
EVT::getEVT(Type *Ty)  — IR type → Extended Value Type
  [llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp, llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h]
  ↓
TargetLowering::getValueType(DataLayout, Type *)  — pointer-aware IR→EVT
  [llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h]
  ↓
ComputeValueTypes / ComputeValueVTs  — aggregate decomposition
  [llvm/lib/CodeGen/Analysis.cpp, llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/Analysis.h]
  ↓
SelectionDAGBuilder::visitLoad/visitStore/etc.  — IR instructions → DAG nodes with EVT
  [llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp]
  ↓
SelectionDAGISel  — DAG nodes → machine instructions
  [llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGISel.cpp]
```"
}
